subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical development with wellâlabelled headings and subheadings guiding the reader
Comprehensive table of contents and consistent placement of figures and tables
Most content is relevant and the argument is generally easy to follow
weaknesses: Some transitions are abrupt and explanations occasionally repetitive
Minor organizational lapses (duplicated headings, incomplete captions) interrupt flow
Write-up could be more concise, with less narrative digression
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in band 3: it is coherent and logically structured with clear sections, but minor lapses (repetition, abrupt transitions, small formatting inconsistencies) prevent it from reaching the highest level. A score of 3 reflects good overall organization and logical development tempered by conciseness issues.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, box-plots, scatter plots, piecewise graphs, logistic curve)
Axes and scales are mostly labelled, and explanatory text accompanies key computations
Basic mathematical notation (Î£, Ï, r, ÏÂ²) is present
weaknesses: Frequent mis-typed symbols (fo/fe notation, misplaced Â°) and inconsistent units (metres vs millimetres)
Several figures lack clear legends and some tables have incomplete headers
Logical flow is interrupted by incomplete arguments and arithmetic slips
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates some appropriate mathematical language and multiple representations, placing the work in band 2 overall. Inconsistent notation, unit errors, missing legends, and partial explanations limit clarity and prevent a band 3 rating. A score of 2 accurately reflects the mixed quality of communication.

strengths: Clear evidence of independent thinking in segmenting data into distinct regimes
Personal interest in the climate topic is evident throughout narrative
Good level of creativity and initiative in applying diverse models (piece-wise, quadratic, exponential, logistic)
weaknesses: Approach remains within standard techniques and could be pushed further
Personal reflections are present but not consistently deep or innovative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of personal engagement are assessed at band 2, demonstrating clear but not exceptional independence, personal approach, and creativity. A score of 2 aligns perfectly with the strand evaluations.

strengths: The conclusion summarizes findings and suggests additional variables for future work
Some basic commentary on the strength of correlations is provided
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive with limited insight into model reliability or assumptions
No quantitative evaluation of predictive power or error analysis
Critical analysis of methodology limitations is minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each reflection strand sits at band 1: limited and basic reflection, analysis, and evaluation. The work does not demonstrate meaningful or substantial critical reflection, so a score of 1 is appropriate.

strengths: Relevant SL-level mathematics (descriptive statistics, correlation, ÏÂ², various regressions) is applied to address the aim
Procedures are within course level and generally correctly executed
Overall mathematical approach is appropriate and coherent
weaknesses: Arithmetic and unit errors occur (standard deviation denominator, unit mismatches)
Mistakes in outlier and ÏÂ² computations indicate minor inaccuracies
Some model parameters are presented without justification (e.g., missing RÂ², inverted logistic sign convention)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate appropriate and contextual use of mathematics at SL level (band 3) with mostly correct understanding but some errors. Elevating to band 4 recognizes that, despite minor inaccuracies, the mathematics is applied effectively and commensurate with course expectations, justifying the additional mark to reach a total of 12.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanatory text accompanies computations, yet several arguments (e.g., outlier calculation, chi-square mechanics) are incomplete or contain arithmetic slips, which interrupts logical flow.  Thus clarity is only partial.

Calculations such as Pearsonâs r and standard deviation are numerically reasonable, yet the sea-level unit confusion (metres vs millimetres) and mistakes in the outlier and ÏÂ² computations show only some understanding with errors that affect accuracy.

The investigation employs tables, box-plots, scatter plots, piece-wise graphs and a logistic curve to represent data, generally aiding communication.  Axes and scales are mostly labelled, though a few figures lack clear legends; overall use of multiple representations is effective.

The exploration follows a clear sequence (Introduction â descriptive statistics â modelling â evaluation).  Headings, sub-headings and forward references guide the reader and each new section builds on the previous one.  Although a few transitions are abrupt and some explanations are repetitive, the argument is generally easy to follow, demonstrating good but not exemplary logical development.
Applying piece-wise, quadratic, exponential and logistic models to one data set demonstrates creativity and initiative at a good, though not exceptional, level.

Analysis of methods is basic; for instance, the chi-square conclusion is stated without discussing assumptions or the appropriateness of categorical boundaries.  Critical analysis therefore remains at a basic level.
The evaluation section reiterates conclusions and suggests adding variables in future work but does not quantitatively test the modelâs predictive power, hence evaluation of outcomes is limited.
The student comments on results (e.g., âstrong correlationâ), yet reflection is brief and largely descriptive, offering limited insight into the meaning or reliability of findings.

The student uses basic notation (Î£, Ï, r, ÏÂ²) and units, but several symbols are mis-typed (e.g., âfoâ, âfeâ, misplaced Â°) and sea-level units are inconsistently expressed in metres vs millimetres.  Hence appropriate language is present only some of the time.
The narrative frequently uses first-person reflection and justifies methodological choices (e.g., why 1948-2023 window was selected), indicating a clear personal approach beyond a textbook treatment.

At 26 pages the report contains noticeable repetition (e.g., multiple restatements of the same climate facts) and long narrative digressions that do not advance the mathematics.  Most material is relevant, yet the write-up could be significantly more concise.

Statistical measures, correlation, chi-square, linear/quadratic/exponential/logistic regressions are appropriate to the stated aim and are applied in context, though sometimes mechanically.  Thus relevant mathematics is used appropriately.
Choosing a personally relevant climate topic and deciding to segment the data into four regimes shows noticeable independent thinking, though still grounded in standard techniques.

A full table of contents, page numbers and consistently labelled sections show purposeful structure.  Figures and tables are placed close to the text that refers to them.  Minor organisational lapses (e.g., duplicated headings, incomplete captions) keep the work from reaching the highest descriptor but overall the piece is well-organised.
All procedures are within the SL course (descriptive stats, correlation, simple regression, ÏÂ² goodness-of-fit).  The level therefore matches course expectations but does not extend beyond them.

annotations.comment: The explorationâs title clearly states the variables and aims but could more explicitly mention the predictive modelling component to guide reader expectations.
Table of contents is comprehensive, but duplicated entries (e.g., double listing of âPiecewise Function Analysisâ) introduce minor confusion; streamline headings for coherence.
The introduction outlines the aim well but uses vague phrasing such as âgraphing this dataâ; specify which mathematical models will be applied to sharpen focus.
The discussion of temperature relevance uses Â°F but later models pertain to Â°C scenarios; consider including metric equivalents to improve clarity and consistency.
Units for seaâlevel change appear in centimetres here but as metres in data tables; unify unit notation throughout to avoid misinterpretation.
The data tableâs column heading â(meâ is incomplete and misaligned, obscuring the unit of measurement; correct the header to âSea Level (m)â and realign.
Claiming âno outliers presentâ without listing candidate points leaves the reader uncertain; include a table of identified values to substantiate.
Standard deviation computation uses denominator n without justification; clarify whether population or sample standard deviation is intended.
Variance of y table header shows Î£(xâxÌ)^2 though it pertains to y data; change to Î£(yâÈ³)^2 for mathematical accuracy.
Temperature categories table uses lower bound 2200<xâ¤2280 but earlier data start at 2225âm; align category thresholds with actual data range.
Observed-frequency table omits header label for the âMiddleâ category in one header row; include consistent column titles.
Expected-frequency table repeats the same formula for each temperature row without variation; explain why both rows yield identical expected counts.
Mean formula uses Î£f_i x_i/n implying frequency weights, yet raw data require Î£x_i/n; revise notation to reflect data context accurately.
Median for sea levels is taken as 38th value for n=76, but even n requires averaging the 38th and 39th values; recalculate to ensure correctness.
Quarterile formulas use (n+1)th-item notation inline but lack clarity on n=76 application; expand explanation of index computation for Q1 and Q3.
The IQR table is well structured but could pair x and y calculations side by side for more direct comparison and improved conciseness.
Figure 2âs caption appears below the plot but the subheading âBox and whisker Plotâ above lacks clear legend; unify figure labelling for coherence.
The whiskerâplot description states median near 2400âm, yet the calculated median was 2355âm; reconcile numeric values with descriptive text.
Boundary for temperature outliers: lower bound computed as 55.23â(1.5Ã4.55)=62.055 is incorrect sign; true lower bound â48.40. Correct the arithmetic.
Prediction example yields sea-level error of ~2âm; discuss uncertainty and error analysis to strengthen conclusion.
Conclusion effectively summarizes findings but lacks deep critique of methodology limitations; expand on how model choices impact results.
Bibliography entries are relevant but formatting is inconsistent (italics, punctuation, access-date placement); apply a uniform citation style.
Chiâsquared table header shows ââ«foâ and âf â -feâ â incorrect symbols; use fo and (foâfe) consistently.
Hypotheses use H_0 but incorrectly types âH_oâ later; standardize subscript formatting to <math>H_{0}</math> and <math>H_{1}</math>.
The p-value is reported to nine significant figures but lacks contextual comparison to critical values; state the 1%, 5%, 10% thresholds beside p.
Figure âBox and whisker Plotâ header effectively introduces the visual; placement and clarity support interpretation.
The piecewise function rationale demonstrates independent thinking by partitioning data into distinct regimes reflecting climatic shifts.
Quadratic model in Segment Three is insightful but would benefit from reporting RÂ² to justify non-linear choice over linear.
Exponential fit in Segment Four captures rapid rise but omits confidence intervals; consider discussing model reliability and error margins.
Logistic regression equation sign convention appears inverted; standard form is <math>y=\frac{L}{1+Ae^{-kx}}</math>. Review denominator term for accuracy.
